title: Valerianella woodsiana

This family is part of the order Dipsacales, a group of plants with showy flowers and opposite leaves. The order Dipsacales is a part of the clade Asterids, which also includes other flowering plants such as mint, honeysuckle, and snapdragon. The clade Asterids is a subgroup of the larger clade Angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. Valerianella woodsiana is a member of the genus Valerianella, a group of small, annual herbs.

intro: Valerianella woodsiana, also known as Woods' corn salad, is a species of flowering plant in the family Valerianaceae, commonly found in Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce stress, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Valerianella woodsiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
